title: Co-Design, Equity, and Accessible Participation · Product Forensics
summary: A preserved decision record for co-design, equity, and accessible participation contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in co-design plan and equity impact record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.
Budget format, assistive technology, language, schedule, compensation, connectivity, cognitive load, and support accommodations before recruitment.
Compare benefits, burdens, error rates, exclusion, recourse, and resource demands across relevant groups without inventing demographic certainty.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Participation Level
steps.instruction:
debug case — Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.

Inspect the Participation Level trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Participation Level.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Participation Level, keep Participation Level connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Participation Level, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Participation Level, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Participation Level precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Participation Level, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Participation Level, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Calling a session co-design grants participants decision power.

stimulus.title: Participation Level case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Calling a session co-design grants participants decision power.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence. Artifact under review: co-design plan and equity impact record.

options.text: The Participation Level record says co-design plan and equity impact record looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Participation Level.

For inspect the failure record, apply Participation Level without its stated preconditions, omit the co-design plan and equity impact record trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Participation Level for co-design plan and equity impact record.
The record names the assumption, applies Participation Level, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Feedback Accountability
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Feedback Accountability: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The debug variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Feedback Accountability.
steps.why: Retrieving Feedback Accountability through the concrete co-design plan and equity impact record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Feedback Accountability, keep Feedback Accountability connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.
